statement: The v0 audit layer should use a metadata-first, append-only `llm_attempts` design split across a compact attempt envelope, a separate payload/blob reference shape, and an append-only quota-event shape, with `provider_extensions` carrying provider-wire details and first-class columns limited to cross-provider operational needs.
rationale: This is the smallest schema that keeps idempotency, retention, provider reconciliation, and quota auditing explicit without running into Convex document limits or forcing provider-wire details into core columns.
keyAssumptions: The team is willing to accept a slightly more explicit multi-shape design instead of a single oversized attempts table
Reproducibility depends on preserving render-input references and hashes, not only raw payload blobs
The attempt ledger remains append-only and separate from scientific artifact dedupe
validationCriteria: Real LLM payload sizes fit the storage model without depending on giant inline docs
Ambiguous provider outcomes can be reconciled via first-class correlation ids
Quota reservations and refunds remain auditable per attempt
critiquePoints: Schema sprawl is a real complexity cost
Replay guarantees weaken if render inputs are later redacted or lost
questions: Which provider-specific fields deserve promotion out of `provider_extensions` in v0 versus later?
